sections.question:
Mr. McFerrick purchased a ticket to the Brown for Senate fundraiser. Is this a reportable item on Mr. McFerrick's lobbyist statement since it is a contribution to a federal committee?

ANSWER: Regulation 18G16 (d)(1)(A) states that "All monetary & non-monetary contributions of $25 or more made from the lobbyist's personal funds to state candidates, elected state officers, & committees supporting such candidates" shall be reported on the lobbyist's report. Brown is an elected state officer & will be at least until the end of 1982 so contributions to any committees under
sections.conclusion: Regulation 18G16 (d)(1)(A) states that "All monetary & non-monetary contributions of $25 or more made from the lobbyist's personal funds to state candidates, elected state officers, & committees supporting such candidates" shall be reported on the lobbyist's report. Brown is an elected state officer & will be at least until the end of 1982 so contributions to any committees under
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a lobbyist's purchase of a ticket to a fundraiser for an elected state officer's federal campaign committee constitutes a reportable contribution on the lobbyist's statement.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The contribution is reportable under Regulation 18G16(d)(1)(A) because Brown is an elected state officer, and contributions of $25 or more from a lobbyist's personal funds to committees supporting such officers must be reported on the lobbyist's statement.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document appears to be internal FPPC routing memo with embedded advice letter content. Conclusion section is incomplete/cut off. Date discrepancy: memo dated 12/4/78 but document header indicates 1982. Respondent names and routing information suggest this is pre-advice review stage.
